purpose: Source and freeze verified public ANNOUNCEMENT timestamps for the S&P 500 addition census, to unblock the announcement->effective continuation test. Frozen BEFORE searching; joined to returns only AFTER the manifest and rejection ledger are frozen.

blindness_protocol.enumerate: ALL 113 addition effective-date batches, never a convenient subset. The worklist fixes the denominator before searching.
blindness_protocol.no_returns_while_sourcing: Do not calculate or inspect any return, residual, or price outcome while sourcing announcement times.
blindness_protocol.freeze_before_join: Freeze the accepted announcement manifest AND the rejection ledger before joining to any market outcome.

source_tiers.1: Timestamped S&P Dow Jones Indices release (spglobal.com / spdji.com).
source_tiers.2: Timestamped release distributed through an official newswire (PR Newswire / Business Wire / GlobeNewswire) carrying the S&P DJI release.
source_tiers.3: Reliable contemporaneous reporting that quotes the announcement with a defensible publication time (e.g. Reuters/Bloomberg/WSJ timestamped story).

reject_rules: INFERRED_TIME: any time estimated, back-calculated, or assumed (e.g. 'usually after close').
ARTICLE_UPDATE_TIME: a story's last-updated time rather than its original publication time.
DATE_ONLY: a date with no defensible publication time.
NON_CONTEMPORANEOUS: a retrospective list/database entry that is not the original release or contemporaneous report.

field_rules.announcement_public_time: ISO-8601 with explicit offset; the moment the release/report first became public.
field_rules.announcement_timezone: IANA tz of the source (e.g. America/New_York).
field_rules.source_sha256: sha256 of the fetched source document, stored for audit.
field_rules.affected_tickers: Subset of the batch's added tickers this announcement covers.
field_rules.timestamp_precision: exact_minute | exact_hour ; anything coarser is DATE_ONLY and rejected.

entry_timing_rule.principle: Tradable entry is the first executable observation AFTER publication. Added names are mid-caps with daily bars only, so the executable entry is a daily session open, never an intraday fill.
entry_timing_rule.INTRADAY: Announcement during regular session -> first_executable_time = next session open (no intraday fill without minute bars).
entry_timing_rule.AFTER_HOURS: Announcement outside regular session -> first_executable_time = next session open.
entry_timing_rule.DATE_ONLY: No defensible publication time -> BLOCKED from the continuation test (kept in the census, excluded from the tradable subset).

deliverables: announcement_worklist_v1.jsonl (all 113 addition batches, UNRESOLVED) -- frozen denominator
tier and precision coverage counts by batch, year, change_type
gate: Report how many of the 113 addition batches reach a VERIFIED Tier 1/2/3 timestamp. That verified-and-covered subset -- not 113 -- is the tradable continuation sample. If it is small, the continuation test is a descriptive pilot; do not relax the tiers.
